question: What are the three incremental strategies in dbt and when do you use each?
answer: append: no deduplication, just inserts new rows. Fastest but only safe for truly append-only event data. merge: SQL MERGE upsert using unique_key. Standard choice for mutable data. delete+insert: deletes matched rows then re-inserts, used on warehouses without native MERGE support or when merge has performance issues. The gotcha: not all strategies are available on all adapters (e.g., Snowflake supports merge natively; BigQuery uses merge; Redshift historically used delete+insert).
